reviews.name: Karen M.
reviews.text: We were overwhelmed trying to help my mother qualify for long-term care. The team walked us through Medicaid planning with patience and clarity, and we finally felt like we had a plan.

reviews.name: Thomas & Ellen R.
reviews.text: We put off our estate plan for years. Heritage Elder Law Group made the process calm, organized, and far easier than we expected. We left with real peace of mind.

reviews.name: Melissa D.
reviews.text: After my father passed away, probate felt impossible to navigate alone. They explained each step, kept us informed, and handled everything with compassion.

reviews.name: George P.
reviews.text: We needed guardianship for an aging parent and did not know where to begin. They were steady, responsive, and thoughtful through the entire process.

reviews.name: William H.
reviews.text: As a veteran, I was not sure what benefits my family could access. They helped us understand the options and put a plan in place that actually made a difference.

faq.question: What is elder law?
faq.answer: Elder law focuses on the legal and financial issues that affect older adults and their families, including estate planning, long-term care planning, Medicaid, probate, guardianship, and veterans benefits.

faq.question: When should I start planning?
faq.answer: Earlier is better. Planning before a health crisis gives your family more options, less stress, and more time to make thoughtful decisions.

faq.question: How does Medicaid planning work?
faq.answer: Medicaid planning looks at assets, income, care needs, and timing to help families protect what they can while preparing for long-term care costs within the rules.

faq.question: What's the difference between a will and a trust?
faq.answer: A will says how assets should be handled after death, while a trust can manage assets during life and after death, often with more control, privacy, and probate avoidance.

faq.question: How much does a consultation cost?
faq.answer: We offer a free initial consultation so families can understand their options and decide on the right next step before making a commitment.

faq.question: Can you help with VA benefits?
faq.answer: Yes. We help eligible veterans and families understand benefits planning and how it may fit into a broader long-term care strategy.
